title: Symplectic embeddings and infinite staircases
speaker: Ana Rita Pires
abstract: McDuff and Schlenk studied an embedding capacity function, which describes when a 4-dimensional ellipsoid can symplectically embed into a 4-ball. The graph of this function includes an infinite staircase determined by the odd index Fibonacci numbers. Infinite staircases have also been shown to exist in the graphs of the embedding capacity functions when the target manifold is a polydisk or the ellipsoid $E(2,3)$.

proof_stages.label: Embedding-capacity problem and known staircases
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the symplectic embedding problem, defines ellipsoid-to-target capacity functions, reviews ECH-capacity obstructions in basic ellipsoid cases, and uses the Fibonacci/Pell-type known cases to motivate the search for further infinite staircases.

proof_stages.label: ECH capacities and toric-domain framework
proof_stages.summary: ECH capacities are introduced as monotone obstructions to four-dimensional symplectic embeddings, and Cristofaro-Gardiner's concave-to-convex toric-domain setting reduces the search to convex toric targets amenable to ball-packing and capacity computations.

proof_stages.label: Twelve reflexive toric-domain theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated: among the displayed reflexive-polytopal toric domains, twelve convex toric domains have ellipsoid-embedding capacity functions with infinite staircases, while the remaining displayed polygons are excluded or not toric domains in the required sense.

proof_stages.label: Predicted accumulation and recursive corner data
proof_stages.summary: For the new staircases the expected accumulation point is expressed using the larger root of a quadratic involving the normalized volume, and the conjectural exterior corners are described by ratios from recursive integer sequences found computationally.

proof_stages.label: Ehrhart comparison proving the accumulation value
proof_stages.summary: The proof uses the piecewise-linear/volume-constraint structure of the capacity function and compares ECH-capacity counting functions with Ehrhart quasipolynomials for suitable triangles to show that the capacity function hits the volume constraint at the predicted accumulation point.

proof_stages.label: Excluding the finite alternatives
proof_stages.summary: Immediately to the left of the accumulation point the function cannot remain on the volume curve, because candidate exterior-corner obstructions lift it, and it cannot be a single linear segment, by further ECH-capacity arguments, so it must have infinitely many staircase steps.

proof_stages.label: Conjectural classification by Pick's theorem
proof_stages.summary: A proposed uniqueness argument derives a necessary perimeter-volume equation for any infinite staircase, argues that sufficiently complex behavior should force a quasipolynomial Ehrhart condition, rescales to perimeter equals volume, and then uses Pick's theorem to force exactly one interior lattice point, explaining the reflexive-polytopal list.
